question: Are there differences in purchase conversion rates between genders?

analysis.chart_type: Violin plot (distribution of conversion likelihood by gender and age)
analysis.design_rationale: A violin plot shows the distribution of purchase conversion likelihood (PurchaseStatus, binary) across gender while also mapping age for deep segmentation. By plotting 'Age' distributions separately for converted vs non-converted in each gender, it illuminates whether gendered conversion differences are consistent across age ranges or whether certain age-gender intersections drive higher conversion. This enables a nuanced view beyond simple averages.
analysis.target_insight: How differences in purchase conversion rates between genders interact with age segmentations, showcasing whether conversion disparities are uniform across age or concentrated in certain demographics.

chart_encodings.y: Age (distribution within each gender and conversion group)
chart_encodings.color: PurchaseStatus (within each gender, lighter/darker color for conversion or not)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Violins for purchase status=1 and purchase status=0 side-by-side for each gender, alpha blending, legend for conversion status

transformations: Filter df to valid ages (18-70), group by Gender and PurchaseStatus, extract Age distributions
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\63.csv')
df_age = df[(df['Age'] >= 18) & (df['Age'] <= 70)]
genders = [0,1]
status = [0,1]
data = []
for g in genders:
    for s in status:
        ages = df_age[(df_age['Gender']==g) & (df_age['PurchaseStatus']==s)]['Age'].values
        data.append(ages)
positions = [0,1,3,4]  # [Male_no, Male_yes, Female_no, Female_yes]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,5))
cmap = mpl.colormaps['Set2'].resampled(2)
violin_parts = ax.violinplot(data, positions=positions, widths=0.7, showmeans=True, showextrema=True, showmedians=True)
for i, pc in enumerate(violin_parts['bodies']):
    if i%2==0:
        pc.set_facecolor(cmap(0))
        pc.set_alpha(0.4)
    else:
        pc.set_facecolor(cmap(1))
        pc.set_alpha(0.7)
for part in ['cbars','cmedians','cmeans']:
    vp = violin_parts[part]
    vp.set_edgecolor('black')
    vp.set_linewidth(1.2)
ax.set_xticks([0.5,3.5])
ax.set_xticklabels(['Male','Female'])
ax.set_xlabel('Gender')
ax.set_ylabel('Age')
ax.set_xlim(-1,5)
legend_elements = [
    mpl.patches.Patch(facecolor=cmap(0), alpha=0.4, label='Not Purchased'),
    mpl.patches.Patch(facecolor=cmap(1), alpha=0.7, label='Purchased')
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, title='PurchaseStatus', loc='best')
plt.title('Age Distribution by Gender and Purchase Conversion Status')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
